key_events.description: Technique executed on a GitHub Actions ephemeral Windows VM. Ephemeral runners are isolated per-job; no persistent outbound C2 or lateral movement observed across execution window.

sigma.title: Potential Initial Access via DLL Search Order Hijacking
Regsvr32 DLL Execution With Uncommon Extension
Potential Registry Persistence Attempt Via DbgManagedDebugger

osquery.name: t1574_008_crontab
osquery.query_text: SELECT event, minute, hour, day_of_month, month, day_of_week, command, path FROM crontab;

osquery.name: t1574_008_startup
osquery.query_text: SELECT name, path, source, status, type FROM startup_items;

osquery.name: t1574_008_suid
osquery.query_text: SELECT path, username, groupname, permissions FROM suid_bin;

sigma_matches.title: Potential Initial Access via DLL Search Order Hijacking
Regsvr32 DLL Execution With Uncommon Extension
Potential Registry Persistence Attempt Via DbgManagedDebugger

story.narrative: Execution, telemetry, detections, artifacts, network observations, and SOAR response are tracked as separate witnesses and tied together through shared anchors.

promotion.reason: Full S5 validation (Linux Docker sandbox): 3 direct run(s), 6/6 Sigma rules matched with event linkage, 0 osquery correlations confirmed, raw telemetry preserved, analyst reviewed, clean baseline.

integrity.hash_method: SHA-256 of canonical JSON (sort_keys=True, default=str) excluding the integrity block itself
